# RMW Commerce — Rick Watson's public publications

> Rick Watson is an ecommerce consultant, founder of RMW Commerce Consulting,
> and host of The Watson Weekly. This repository is his canonical public
> publication home: long-form guides, reference docs, and runnable Claude
> Code skills on ecommerce strategy, Claude usage, agent design, and the
> discoverability standards that make all of it citable. The headline
> reference here is the Marketing to Agents playbook, which documents how
> these very pages aim to be readable, citable, and operable across every
> class of AI agent.

## Guides

- [Marketing to Agents](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/marketing-to-agents.md): The authoritative playbook for making any website readable, citable, and operable across every class of AI agent — indexer bots, AI search surfaces, chat assistants, and agentic browsers. Every claim sourced; 22-item checklist impact-ordered by evidence weight.
- [The 5-Minute Claude Code Test](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-5-minute-test.md): For current Claude.ai users deciding whether Claude Code is worth installing. One paste-able meta-prompt that audits a real conversation from your chat history and returns a personalized YES/NO with three concrete differences if YES.
- [Your First Project: A Paste-Along Tutorial (for Non-Developers)](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-non-developers-first-tutorial.md): The canonical "I'm starting" doc for non-developers. Install steps at the top, then a 45-minute paste-along to a working one-page personal site, with a deliberate break-and-recover exercise that locks the approval loop into muscle memory.
- [Make Your Work Survive Your Laptop: Your First Private GitHub Repo](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-non-developers-github-tutorial.md): The natural session 2 after the first-project tutorial. 30-minute paste-along that installs `gh`, authenticates, creates a private repo, and rehearses the "I lost my laptop" recovery by deliberately deleting the local folder and cloning it back.
- [Claude Code for Non-Developers: A Field Guide](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-for-non-developers.md): The why behind the tutorials — mindset shift, the seven question templates, the failure modes, and the two-gate rule for outbound communications. Read after the first-project and GitHub tutorials. Written by a non-developer who runs a 20+ skill agent system daily.
- [Claude Code Workflow Optimizer](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-optimizer.md): Patterns for reducing token waste and rework in Claude Code, ranked by ROI. Context-window discipline, CLAUDE.md hygiene, verification-first workflow, model routing, and worktree fleets — all grounded in Anthropic's official documentation.
- [Making Claude Respond Faster](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/making-claude-faster.md): Prompt caching, model selection, parallel tool calls, and the patterns that slow you down. 17 sources across primary docs, Anthropic engineering, independent practitioners, and peer-reviewed research.
- [The Prompts-to-Agents Ladder](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/the-prompts-to-agents-ladder.md): When to stay with a prompt, when to package a skill, when to build an agent, and when a multi-agent system is actually warranted. The on-ramp before the multi-agent guide.
- [Multi-Agent Fan-Out and Verification](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/multi-agent-fan-out-and-verification.md): How to structure Claude agent systems that scale without silently breaking. Typed return contracts, intermediate-state logging, thin-orchestrator architecture, and a concrete phasing strategy.
- [Why Is Claude Code So Noisy? What to Do About It](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-code-noise.md): Your terminal looks like a wall of text by design. Explains the four sources of noise and ranks the tactics for filtering what matters from what's just volume.
- [Claude Permissions: Stop the Interruption Hell](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/claude-permissions-guide.md): The interruption problem isn't your imagination. Covers the permission model across claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and Claude Code, with starter defaults and a drift-consolidation strategy.
- [Configuring Claude Code permissions: real lessons vs. cargo cult](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/configuring-claude-code-permissions.md): What the built-in read-only allowlist covers, what patterns are cargo cult, and when hooks and sandboxing are better tools than hand-rolled rules. 13 cited sources.
- [AI Agent Discoverability and Protocols](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/ai-discoverability-and-protocols.md): Concept by concept on the AI-agent discoverability and protocol stack — what's deployable, what's wait-and-see, what's already dead. Includes audience-specific recommendations and direct attribution of hype.
- [Building Excellent Software (with Claude)](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/building-excellent-software-with-claude.md): The tokenmin v0.12.1 → v0.12.5 case study — seven disciplines for taking a Claude-built artifact from working to production-grade. Grounded in the public issue backlog and test suite.
- [Operating Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides from Claude](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/operating-google-workspace-from-claude.md): Patterns, hard limits, and anti-patterns of writing to Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Slides from an AI agent. Workarounds for what the APIs simply cannot do (Cowork Shared Drive connector regression, PAGE_NUMBER auto-text limitation, non-native files like `.docx` / PDF, `valueInputOption=USER_ENTERED` for Sheets, Slides object-ID instability after editor edits, the under-served Slides MCP surface), plus the single-writer agent pattern with resume-from-interruption recovery. 60-second map → what's possible → what's NOT possible → priority + TL;DR at the top; eight-part deep dive.
- [GA4 with Claude — Data API, BigQuery, and the Multi-Source Warehouse](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/ga4-with-claude.md): Which of GA4's four surfaces to point an agent at, when the Data API is enough and when you need the BigQuery export, the observed-vs-modeled distinction that explains most agent errors, read-only wiring with cost guardrails, and an honest assessment of which connectors actually deliver the native export.
- [YouTube Analytics with Claude — Two Analytics APIs, Not One](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/youtube-analytics-with-claude.md): Which of YouTube's three APIs answers your question — the Data API v3 for metadata and public counts, the Analytics API for ad-hoc queries and the audience-retention curve, and the Reporting API for recurring bulk work. Covers the no-service-account constraint that silently breaks unattended agents, how to solve it once with a persisted refresh token, and why BigQuery's Data Transfer Service is the cleanest setup for anything recurring.
- [RMW Commerce Glossary](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/glossary.md): Every named concept across all guides defined once, with hype vs. reality marked per entry. Organized by category; deep-linkable by anchor.
- [Bringing Five Whys into the Agentic Era](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/bringing-five-whys-into-the-agentic-era.md): The upgrade to linear root-cause analysis that survives the standard objections: branch instead of chain, validate every causal link counterfactually, dispatch an independent AI skeptic whose only job is to break the analysis. Method assembled from Allspaw, Cook, the STELLA report, and Google SRE; worked through a real outage where the skeptic caught the second silent-death path the first pass missed.
- [Designing an E-Commerce Taxonomy Your AI Agents Can Maintain](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/guides/ecommerce-taxonomy-for-ai-agents.md): Why inherited industry-code lists rot the moment AI agents start writing them, and the seven design decisions — Ecosystem Role axis, orthogonal facets, scope notes, stable IDs, provenance, graph layer, one-seam tagging — that prevent classification drift across multi-agent pipelines.

## Skills

- [Marketing-to-Agents skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/marketing-to-agents): Runs the 22-item Marketing-to-Agents checklist against an actual site and returns a gap report.
- [Prompts-to-Agents Ladder skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/prompts-to-agents-ladder): Applies the 4-rung ladder to a workflow and recommends the right rung.
- [Claude Code Optimizer skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/claude-code-optimizer): Audits a Claude Code workflow and prescribes one high-leverage fix.
- [Multi-Agent Fan-Out skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/multi-agent-fan-out): Designs or audits a multi-agent system before building.
- [Claude Code for Non-Developers skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/claude-code-for-non-developers): Coaches a non-developer through ongoing Claude Code work.
- [Claude Code First Project skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/claude-code-first-project): Walks a first-time non-developer through the canonical first-project tutorial — install check, safety basics, building a one-page HTML site, the approval loop, and the deliberate break-and-recover exercise.
- [Claude Token Optimization skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/claude-token-optimization): Audits Claude API or Claude Code token spend and recommends one concrete cost reduction.
- [Building Excellent Software skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/building-excellent-software): Applies the excellent-software checklist to a Claude-built artifact and surfaces the highest-leverage gap before shipping.
- [Operating Google Workspace from Claude skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/operating-google-workspace-from-claude): Audits a Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides automation against the patterns checklist; diagnoses the "works in prompt, fails in skill" connector failure mode, the Sheets `USER_ENTERED` gotcha, the Slides object-ID-after-editor-edit gotcha, and non-native file mishandling.
- [understand-failure skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/understand-failure): Runs a full adversarial Five Whys — branching cause tree tagged by layer (proximate / contributing / systemic), counterfactual verdict on every link, independent skeptic pass with five specific challenges, and system-level owner-assignable remediations. Companion to "Bringing Five Whys into the Agentic Era."
- [ecommerce-taxonomy skill](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/tree/main/skills/ecommerce-taxonomy): Audits or designs a faceted e-commerce entity taxonomy — diagnoses classification drift, drafts vocabulary values with scope notes, writes deterministic conflict-resolution rules for multi-agent setups. Companion to "Designing an E-Commerce Taxonomy Your AI Agents Can Maintain."

## Optional

- [Token Optimization for Claude — full guide pointer](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/tokenmin-optimization-pointer.md): A short pointer to the complete token-optimization guide at tokenmin.ai plus the runnable skill.
- [README](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/README.md): Repository index, business links, and the canonical list of every guide and skill.
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/watsonrm/rmwcommerce/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Conventions and publishing process for AI agents and human contributors working in this repository.
